prompt:
A researcher claims that average commute in the metro area fell steadily from 2020 to 2023.

Which statement is best supported by the data in the table?

choices.text: In some years the value moved up and in others down, rather than in a single direction.
choices.rationale: Every year moves the same direction, so this misreads the table.

choices.text: The value changed by the same amount every year, a constant 6 minutes per year.
choices.rationale: The year-to-year changes vary, so the change was not a constant 6 minutes.

choices.text: It peaked in 2021 at 126 minutes and then reversed course for the rest of the period.
choices.rationale: The largest value is at an endpoint, not the middle year.

choices.text: The value dropped each year, moving from 138 to 112 minutes over the period.
choices.rationale: Correct. This matches the steady, one-direction trend the claim describes across every year shown.

explanation: The supporting statement must reflect the table's actual, consistent trend across all the years shown.

table.caption: Average commute in the metro area (minutes)
